Life on Earth began with microorganisms approximately 3.8 billion years ago. Studying microbial evolution gives us a window into the origins of all life, including the emergence of complex multicellular organisms. Evolution microbiology

Microorganisms are either prokaryotic (no nucleus, like bacteria) or eukaryotic (with nucleus, like fungi and protozoa). Understanding their cell structure reveals how life organizes itself at the most fundamental level. Cell Structure microbiology
